title: ConvertKit vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Omnisend. We break down pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs.
intro: ConvertKit and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms with different strengths. ConvertKit excels for creators and course sellers, while Omnisend is purpose-built for e-commerce brands. Your choice depends on whether you need email-focused features or omnichannel marketing capabilities.
choose_a_if: You're a content creator, blogger, or online course seller looking for email-focused tools
You need generous free tier limits to test before paying ($0-1,000 subscribers)
You want robust landing pages and commerce features built into one platform
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need email, SMS, and push notifications together
You want more affordable pricing starting at $16/month instead of $29
You need advanced segmentation and pre-built automation workflows for retail

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is superior for creators and content professionals, while Omnisend excels for e-commerce businesses. Your best choice depends on your business model and whether you need SMS and push notifications alongside email.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend is more affordable, starting at $16/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month entry price. However, ConvertKit offers a larger free tier (1,000 vs 250 subscribers), making it better for bootstrapped creators testing the platform.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list exports, making migration possible. However, you'll lose SMS and push notification data, and may need to recreate automations manually since the platforms use different workflow builders. Plan for some setup time during the transition.
